question: Why is natural latex better than memory foam?

children.text: Natural latex is highly elastic, bounces back instantly, breathes cool, is hypoallergenic, and has zero VOCs — none of which is true of memory foam. Open-cell Dunlop-processed latex has natural ventilation channels that dissipate body heat 2–3°C cooler than foam.

question: Is a latex mattress good for back pain?

children.text: Yes. A quality latex mattress provides balanced firm support with pressure relief, maintaining the natural S-curve of the spine — widely recommended by physiotherapists across South India. Medium-firm models like Arogya are specifically designed for orthopedic support.

question: How much does a natural latex mattress cost in India?

children.text: At RelaxPro, entry-level models start from ₹9,500 (single size) and go up to ₹54,000 (king, premium pure latex). Most customers find their ideal model between ₹25,000–₹45,000. Custom sizes are quoted via WhatsApp.

question: What does GOLS certified really mean?

children.text: GOLS (Global Organic Latex Standard) requires at least 95% organic raw material from sustainably managed rubber plantations, with strict waste management and chemical usage standards. Every RelaxPro core carries this certification.

No — natural latex sleeps significantly cooler than memory foam. The Dunlop-processed open-cell structure allows air circulation that dissipates body heat. Quilted organic cotton covers further enhance breathability.

5 standard sizes: Single (48×75), Diwan (48×75), Double (60×75), Queen (60×78), King (72×78). Custom sizes available in any dimension from 48"–96" L × 24"–84" W. Custom pricing on request via WhatsApp.

question: Is the mattress too firm? Can I get a softer feel?

children.text: Natural latex comes across a range of comfort levels. Plush lovers should look at Nirvana or Ananda. Medium-soft models (Amrita, Prakriti, Somya) add HR softy or rebonded foam transition layers for extra contouring.

question: How do I care for and maintain my latex mattress?

children.text: Flip head-to-foot every 3 months (first year), then every 6 months. Spot-clean the organic cotton cover with mild soap — no machine wash. Vacuum monthly with upholstery attachment. With proper care, expect 12–15 years of use.
